Definition: parcellated brain-region abbreviation as printed in the Figure 3 legend
Note: 13 regions; key: Fr frontal, Pa parietal, Te temporal, Oc occipital, Ce cerebellum, Sm sensorimotor; SM superior+middle, I inferior, O orbitofrontal, SI superior+inferior, TP temporo-parietal junction, A anterior, P posterior, V vermis

Definition: canonical lobe / structure the region belongs to
Note: FrontalLobe, ParietalLobe, TemporalLobe, OccipitalLobe, Cerebellum, SensorimotorCortex

Definition: sub-region within the structure (from the legend key)
species of the reference group whose mean volumes are reported
comparison group for the reported means
Note: MH (modern H. sapiens); the study also compares NT (Neanderthal, n=4) and EH (early H. sapiens, n=4) but their values are only shown as bars, not in text

Definition: number of modern-human individuals contributing to the MH means
Note: inferred from ANOVA df (2,1190): MH=1185, NT=4, EH=4

Definition: mean modern-human volume of the parcellated region (as printed)
Note: reconstructed via computational neuroanatomy (GM+WM); printed in cc

Definition: standard deviation of the modern-human region volume (as printed)
mean modern-human region volume converted to project units (mm3)
standard deviation of region volume in project units (mm3)
relative volume of the region (panel a normalization reference)
Note: =1.0 by construction (each region normalized to mean MH volume); NT/EH bars are relative to this but not tabulated

Definition: ANOVA F statistic for the relative-volume difference among NT/EH/MH
Note: df = 2,1190; reported only for the 4 significant regions (Pa SI, Oc SM, Ce V, Ce P)

Definition: ANOVA p-value for the relative-volume difference among NT/EH/MH
whether the region showed a significant relative-volume difference
Note: TRUE for Pa SI / Oc SM / Ce V / Ce P

Definition: fossil and MH brains reconstructed by computational neuroanatomy (DARTEL diffeomorphic deformation of 1,185 living-human MR brains onto endocasts); volumes = GM+WM; size-adjusted by ICV as ANCOVA covariate
